mnemonics.title: Disk Scheduling Algorithm Order
mnemonics.content: "Friendly Students Scan Cities Looking Carefully" -- FCFS, SSTF, SCAN, C-SCAN, LOOK, C-LOOK. This mnemonic helps remember all six disk scheduling algorithms in order of increasing sophistication.

mnemonics.title: SCAN vs LOOK Difference
mnemonics.content: "SCAN goes to the wall, LOOK just looks ahead." SCAN always travels to the end of the disk (the wall). LOOK only goes as far as the last request it can see (looks ahead) and then reverses.

mnemonics.title: C- prefix means Circular
mnemonics.content: "C = Circular = one-way trip." C-SCAN and C-LOOK only service requests in one direction and jump back, treating the disk as a circular structure for uniform wait times.
